question.prompt: Based on the SIEM data so far, are there any users exhibiting unusual access patterns?

ground_truth.canonical_answer: User jmorris is showing expanded file access scope beyond their current project assignment. Download volumes are trending above the user's baseline and peer group average. DLP alerts for jmorris are elevated.
key_facts: lateral information gathering pattern inconsistent with declared operational role
data transfer volumes from a single account diverge from peer group norms in a sustained multi-week pattern
data transfer volumes escalated progressively over a multi-week period with each successive week showing higher peak transfer sizes than the previous
red team exercise alerts are not related to the exfiltration activity
the anomalous data access pattern originates from a single authorized internal account, not from external network intrusion
the data exfiltration uses authorized access paths rather than exploiting software vulnerabilities

answer.answer_text:
**Yes – several users stand out as exhibiting atypical access behavior in the SIEM logs collected so far.** | User | Why the activity is unusual | Supporting evidence | |------|----------------------------|----------------------| | **jmorris** | • Accessed an unusually large number of repositories (9 different repos) in a single day and downloaded the highest number of files (49) with a total volume of ≈ 232 MB – far above the typical 30‑40 files/≈120 MB seen for most users.
• Triggered multiple DLP alerts on the same day (two FILE‑SIZE‑THRESHOLD alerts on PDFs).
• Held a long VPN session (2.4 h) that extended past normal working hours. | 2024‑09‑11 Access Log shows 9 repos and 49 files (127 MB‑231 MB) 【ref_id: insider_threat_05_ep_010】; DLP alerts for jmorris (PDF size thresholds) 【ref_id: insider_threat_05_ep_010】; VPN session of 2.4 h for jmorris 【ref_id: insider_threat_05_ep_010】 | | **sbrown** | • Consistently appears in the “high‑volume” tier (e.g., 36‑40 files per day, > 80 MB) and also accessed many repos (up to 5 in a single day).
• Generated an EXTERNAL‑TRANSFER DLP alert (email attachment of a .dwg file).
• Had the longest VPN session on 9/11 (2.4 h) and a second‑day VPN session lasting > 1 h on 9/09. | 2024‑09‑11 Access Log shows 36 files, 87 MB and multiple repos 【ref_id: insider_threat_05_ep_010】; DLP alert for sbrown (EXTERNAL‑TRANSFER) 【ref_id: insider_threat_05_ep_010】; VPN session 2.4 h for sbrown 【ref_id: insider_threat_05_ep_010】 | | **dlee** | • Frequently hits bulk‑download DLP rules (e.g., on 9/06 and 9/11) indicating large‑scale data extraction.
• Was blocked from using a USB STORAGE device on 9/11, a rare event for most users.
• Shows after‑hours VPN usage (multiple sessions spanning 19:00‑20:00) and a 2‑hour VPN session on 9/13. | Bulk‑download DLP alerts for dlee on 9/06 【ref_id: insider_threat_05_ep_005】 and 9/11 【ref_id: insider_threat_05_ep_010】; USB STORAGE blocked event on 9/11 【ref_id: insider_threat_05_ep_010】; VPN sessions of 1‑2 h after normal hours (e.g., 19:29‑20:17 on 9/06) 【ref_id: insider_threat_05_ep_005】 | | **hkim** (secondary) | • Repeated USB‑PRINTER and USB‑HID blocks across several days, suggesting attempts to use prohibited peripherals.
• Occasionally shows longer VPN sessions (≈1 h) and accesses multiple repos in a short window. | USB‑PRINTER blocked on 9/09 【ref_id: insider_threat_05_ep_008】; USB‑HID blocked on 9/06 【ref_id: insider_threat_05_ep_005】; VPN session 1.1 h on 9/09 【ref_id: insider_threat_05_ep_008】 | **Interpretation** - **High‑volume data pulls** (jmorris, sbrown, dlee) and **multiple repository accesses** in a single day exceed the baseline pattern (most users access 2‑4 repos and download ≤ 40 files/≈120 MB). - **Repeated DLP alerts** (especially bulk‑download or external‑transfer policies) indicate potential data‑exfiltration attempts. - **After‑hours VPN sessions** and **blocked USB device usage** are additional red flags that deviate from normal employee behavior. These users should be prioritized for deeper investigation (e.g., contextual user interviews, review of the specific files accessed, and verification of legitimate business need).

Yes. Across the week‑long data set a handful of users stand out for **far higher download volume and/or a much broader repository‑access scope than the rest of the team**.

| Day (episode) | User | Repos accessed | Volume (MB) | Why it is anomalous |
|---------------|------|----------------|------------|---------------------|
| 2024‑09‑11 (ep 010) | **jmorris** | 9 repos (archive/project‑nova‑2021, project‑sentinel/avionics, project‑atlas/firmware, project‑beacon/signal‑proc, project‑beacon/firmware‑legacy, project‑atlas/test‑harness, project‑atlas/schematics, project‑atlas/integration‑tests, project‑beacon/firmware‑legacy) | 231.7 MB | The only user that day with **≥ 8 repos**; volume is in the top‑quartile (most others ≤ 2‑4 repos, ≤ 180 MB). |
| 2024‑09‑12 (ep 011) | **jmorris** | 12 repos (archive/project‑nova‑2021, project‑beacon/signal‑proc, project‑beacon/firmware‑legacy, shared/documentation, project‑atlas/integration‑tests, project‑atlas/firmware, project‑atlas/test‑harness, project‑atlas/schematics, project‑beacon/rf‑modules, shared/toolchain, archive/project‑nova‑2021/specs, …) | 272.2 MB | Repo count again **well above the peer median (2‑5)** and download volume is the highest of the day. |
| 2024‑09‑13 (ep 012) | **jmorris** | 10 repos (project‑atlas/test‑harness, shared/documentation, project‑atlas/schematics, shared/toolchain, project‑beacon/rf‑modules, project‑sentinel/avionics, project‑atlas/integration‑tests, project‑beacon/signal‑proc, archive/project‑nova‑2021/specs, …) | 374.5 MB | **Largest volume** of any user that week and still accesses **double‑digit repo count**. |
| 2024‑09‑11 (ep 010) | **tchen** | 2 repos | 175.3 MB | Volume is in the top‑10 % while the repo count is typical; the amount of data moved is unusually high for a 2‑repo user. |
| 2024‑09‑12 (ep 011) | **rjohnson** | 5 repos | 164.8 MB | Accesses more repos than the average (≈ 3) and downloads a large amount of data. |
| 2024‑09‑05 (ep 004) | **lpatel** | 3 repos | 177.4 MB | Volume exceeds the median (≈ 120 MB) despite a modest repo count. |
| 2024‑09‑06 (ep 005) | **kwilson** | 2 repos | 150.8 MB | One of the highest download volumes for a 2‑repo user. |
| 2024‑09‑06 (ep 005) | **rjohnson** | 2 repos | 158.6 MB | Same pattern as kwilson – high volume with few repos. |
| 2024‑09‑10 (ep 009) | **sbrown** | 2 repos | 168.5 MB | Very high data transfer for a minimal repo set. |

**Interpretation**

* **jmorris** is the clearest outlier: on three separate days he accesses **9‑12 repositories** and downloads **> 200 MB**, far exceeding the typical 2‑5 repos and ≤ 180 MB seen for most peers. This pattern suggests a **scope anomaly** (broader repository footprint) and a **volume anomaly** (large data movement).  
* Several other users (tchen, rjohnson, kwilson, lpatel, sbrown) show **volume anomalies**—they download > 150 MB while only touching 2‑3 repos, which is atypical compared to the bulk of the team.  

These deviations merit further investigation to determine whether they reflect legitimate work (e.g., large‑binary engineering assets) or potential insider‑threat behavior.
